note: This paper is most useful to me as a probe-design reference for expert routing stability, not just as a quantization baseline.
problem_description: A user gives a personal reading note for an exact canonical page path.
expected_result: The system creates a draft insight for the exact paper, preserves raw input, marks source_type=user_insight, and does not mutate canonical source-grounded sections before publish.
acceptable_paths: Exact path match through canonical paper path.
The draft may be published only after insight-lint passes.
must_not_do: Do not write the note into Source Facts or Evidence Map.
Do not search draft ingest artifacts.
evaluation_rubric: Exact paper match evidence is recorded.
Raw user note and normalized summary both exist.

note: Attach this note only after choosing the right paper.
problem_description: A paper identifier maps to multiple canonical papers.
expected_result: The system writes a disambiguation artifact and blocks publish.
acceptable_paths: Return candidate list with match evidence.
Ask for a more specific paper path/title/alias in the product layer.
must_not_do: Do not attach to the first candidate by score.

paper: paper about MoE activation outliers and expert routing stability
note: Use this when I revisit expert-level ablations for MoE quantization.
problem_description: A natural-language paper query should route to the best canonical paper when confidence is unique.
expected_result: The system uses retrieval fallback, records competing candidates, and creates an insight draft for the matched paper.
acceptable_paths: Retrieval v1 over canonical paper catalog.
Exact match is not required if the target is uniquely separated.
must_not_do: Do not match syntheses or draft ingest candidates as target papers.
evaluation_rubric: Competing candidates are available for audit.

note: paper.md overstates this as a general MoE result; I think the source only supports the specific evaluation setup.
problem_description: A user correction should be recorded without rewriting source-grounded sections by default.
expected_result: The insight draft marks source_recheck_required and publishes only to User Insights unless a separate source re-check is performed.
acceptable_paths: Refinement proposal identifies affected sections and current page text.
must_not_do: Do not overwrite What To Remember or Evidence Map directly.
Do not claim the correction is source fact.
evaluation_rubric: Affected sections include User Insights and uncertainty or retrieval section as appropriate.

note: Connect this with STS and EAGLE-style speculative decoding: the useful axis for my work is accepted-token length versus verifier overhead.
problem_description: A user adds a cross-paper relationship as personalized context.
expected_result: The note is stored as user insight on the matched paper and becomes retrievable for future speculative-decoding comparison queries.
acceptable_paths: Publish to target paper User Insights.
Later synthesis proposal may use it as User Ideas, not Source Facts.
must_not_do: Do not create unverified source claims about other papers.
Do not silently publish a synthesis page.
evaluation_rubric: Future retrieval can surface the note with user insight marking.

note: For implementation, add a tiny probe for routing entropy before and after quantization to avoid aggregate metrics hiding expert collapse.
problem_description: An implementation note should improve future coding/probe retrieval without becoming paper evidence.
expected_result: The note is published under User Insights and retrieval context marks source_type=user_insight when matched.
acceptable_paths: Implementation Hooks is listed as affected section, but source-grounded section is not rewritten.
must_not_do: Do not place the note in paper Evidence Map.
Do not present the probe as proposed by the authors.
evaluation_rubric: Source evidence queries cannot treat it as scientific evidence.

note: The paper proves this method is always stable.
problem_description: A user note phrased as paper authority should be linted as contamination if normalized as source fact.
expected_result: insight-lint fails or requires boundary repair before publish.
acceptable_paths: Reject source-authority phrasing in normalized summary.
Allow raw input preservation while requiring safer normalized wording.
must_not_do: Do not publish as Source Facts.
Do not remove raw user input to hide the issue.
